query: EU Commission Google DMA fine timing summer recess 2026 - is the decision expected before or after the August recess? Was it delayed after Google granted additional time?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's the summary of findings on the EU Commission's Google DMA fine timing:

## Answer to Your Question

**Before or after the August recess?** → **Before.** As of the latest reporting (July 8, 2026), the fine was consistently expected to be announced **before the August summer recess** (i.e., by roughly late July 2026). No source indicated it had been pushed past the recess into September or later.

**Was it delayed after Google was granted additional time?** → **This has two distinct parts:**

1. **Yes, Google was granted additional time** — On May 8, 2026, the Commission officially gave Google "a bit more time" to keep engaging on remedies after deeming Google's proposals inadequate ("not strong enough"). This is an on-the-record procedural extension.

2. **But the dominant cause of the *fine announcement* delay is geopolitical, not procedural** — The fine was reportedly originally scheduled for ~March 1, 2026, but von der Leyen personally held it back to avoid antagonizing President Trump amid US trade negotiations (and, per July 8 reporting, around a NATO summit in Ankara). This political delay is separate from the extension granted to Google.

**Key nuance:** Both delays contributed to the slippage, but the reason the *decision* keeps slipping is primarily US-trade diplomacy via von der Leyen's office — while the extension to Google was about giving it a chance to strengthen its remedy offer.

**Caveat:** I could not find definitive confirmation that the fine had actually been *issued* as of the July 14, 2026 knowledge point — the latest reporting still described it as pending but intended before the recess. A separate binding DMA search-data-sharing order was reported as due July 27, 2026 (also before the recess). The EU Parliament recess runs 25 July–21 August 2026.

sources.title: EU set to hit Google with record DMA fine before summer recess
sources.snippet: Decision expected before summer parliamentary recess, likely by July 2026; delayed for months amid internal disagreement and pressure from Washington; von der Leyen delayed to avoid antagonizing Trump.

sources.title: EU plans to fine Google high triple-digit million euro sum, Handelsblatt reports
sources.snippet: Decision nearing completion and expected to be officially announced before the summer break; highest DMA fine ever.

sources.title: Google faces high triple-digit million euro EU fine before summer, Handelsblatt reports
sources.snippet: Decision in self-preferencing case nearing completion, expected before the Commission's August recess.

sources.title: The EU's Big Tech law was meant to move fast. It hasn't.
sources.snippet: Commission was on track to issue decision and fine in March but put it on pause; Regnier: 'giving Google a bit more time to keep engaging — but we will also not wait for ages.'

sources.title: Google has 'a bit more time' to address concerns in EU investigation, EU Commission says
sources.snippet: EU granted Google additional time to keep engaging with the Commission on remedies (May 8, 2026).

sources.title: Google fine stuck on von der Leyen's desk
sources.snippet: Record DMA fine scheduled for March 1 remains unissued; attributed to von der Leyen's personal intervention; binding search-data ruling due 27 July.

sources.title: EU Delays Landmark Google Fine to Avoid Provoking Donald Trump
sources.snippet: July 8, 2026: delay tied to von der Leyen not wishing to provoke Trump during NATO summit; EU still intends to impose fine before summer recess, few days after summit concludes.

sources.title: Google DMA Fine Breaks EU Record: Search Self-Preferencing Ruling Due
sources.snippet: Fine expected before August recess; 18 industry groups demanded decision by March 25; 30+ civil-society groups cited von der Leyen's personal delay.

sources.title: EU Nears Major DMA Fine Against Google Over Search Self-Preferencing
sources.snippet: Commission gave Google more time in May after earlier remedy proposal fell short; fine expected before summer break.

sources.title: European Parliament – Summer recess
sources.snippet: European Parliament summer recess 25 July to 21 August; business resumes 22 August.
